title: TPU v1 · Synthesis Overview: Translating and Optimizing a 256×256 Array into a 28 nm Gate Netlist
oneLine: Logic synthesis is not a one-click RTL-to-gates conversion. It reads and elaborates RTL, maps generic logic to a 28 nm library, optimizes timing/area/power, and emits a gate netlist plus reports. SDC constraints drive the entire convergence loop for TPU's regular 65,536-MAC array.

legend.name: ① Read RTL
legend.desc: The synthesizer reads signed-off synthesizable RTL: TPU PEs with 8-bit multiply and 32-bit accumulation, the 256×256 array, Unified Buffer control, and CISC decode FSM. This stage parses syntax and hierarchy; no gates exist yet.

legend.name: ② Elaborate (infer generic logic)
legend.desc: Expand parameters and generate constructs—N=256 instantiates the full array—and infer technology-independent adders, multipliers, registers, and multiplexers such as GTECH primitives. Structure is now defined but not tied to a real library.

legend.name: ③ Technology mapping
legend.desc: Bind a 28 nm Liberty standard-cell library and replace generic operators with available NANDs, DFFs, full adders, buffers, and other physical cells. Cell selection directly determines area, delay, and power.

legend.name: ④ Optimize timing / area / power
legend.desc: Restructure under SDC constraints: choose faster or lower-power multi-Vt cells, insert buffers, repair setup/hold, retime logic, and share resources. This is where synthesis balances a 700 MHz target against area and power.

legend.name: SDC constraints drive ②③④
legend.desc: Synthesis is goal-driven. Clock period—illustratively 1.43 ns for ~700 MHz—input/output delay, drive, load, false paths, and multicycle paths determine whether the tool prioritizes timing or efficiency.

legend.name: ⑤ Gate netlist + reports
legend.desc: Outputs include a gate-level netlist of real cells and connectivity, QoR reports for slack, area, and power, plus SDC/SDF for backend work. Reports determine whether the iteration has converged.

steps.cap: ① Read signed-off TPU RTL and elaborate parameters/generate constructs into technology-independent adders, multipliers, registers, and MUXes.
② Bind a 28 nm standard-cell library and, under SDC constraints such as an illustrative 1.43 ns clock, map generic logic to real NAND, DFF, and arithmetic cells.
③ Optimize for 700 MHz timing, area, and power using multi-Vt cell selection, buffering, retiming, and resource sharing.
④ Emit the gate-level netlist, timing/area/power QoR reports, and SDC/SDF for backend implementation.
⑤ If timing, area, or power misses target, revise constraints, RTL, or library strategy and resynthesize until QoR converges.

before: Prepare three input classes: Signed-off synthesizable RTL, the target 28 nm Liberty standard-cell libraries—often with multiple threshold voltages—and SDC constraints. Synthesis cannot proceed credibly without all three.

Write timing constraints: Define the main clock, illustratively create_clock -period 1.43 [get_ports clk] for ~700 MHz, plus I/O delays, drive strength, load, false paths, and multicycle paths. Incorrect constraints optimize the wrong problem.

Declare power intent and DFT requirements: Express multi-voltage or clock-gating intent in UPF/CPF where applicable, and decide whether scan insertion and compression occur during synthesis or later because they alter netlist structure.
Set QoR and library policy: Quantify timing closure at 700 MHz and area/power budgets. Define a multi-Vt policy, typically high-Vt cells for low leakage and low-Vt only on critical paths.
Align process and upstream architecture: Confirm PVT corners and the design parameters being synthesized: 256×256 array, 24 MiB UB, 4 MiB accumulators, and weight-stationary dataflow.
during: Core rule: synthesis is constraint-driven. Tightening the same RTL's clock pushes the tool toward buffers and low-Vt cells at higher area; relaxed constraints favor area and power. Bad SDC produces a bad netlist and meaningless QoR.

Separate translation from optimization. Read, elaborate, and map preserve RTL semantics while progressively selecting concrete cells; optimize changes structure under constraints. Mapping alone is not final quality.

Preserve consistency across the regular array. Elaborating TPU's 256×256 grid creates roughly 65K similar MAC units, which synthesis maps consistently. Critical paths often appear in accumulation chains and inter-PE links.

Use multi-Vt libraries to trade timing for leakage. A common strategy uses high-Vt cells broadly and replaces only failing critical-path cells with faster, leakier low-Vt variants, balancing 700 MHz timing against total power.

Select arithmetic structures carefully. For 8-bit multiply and 32-bit accumulate paths, the tool may choose ripple, carry-lookahead, or tree structures. Tight timing selects faster, larger implementations—a direct area/delay tradeoff.

State methodology provenance: Read→elaborate→map→optimize→netlist, SDC, Liberty, multi-Vt, and QoR are standard EDA synthesis practices used by tools such as Design Compiler, Genus, and Yosys. TPU private scripts, libraries, and QoR are not public; values shown are illustrative.

result.label: Library strategy (example)
result.value: 28 nm multi-Vt .lib: high-Vt baseline / low-Vt critical paths

result.label: Primary output · netlist
result.value: Gate-level netlist.v with real cells and connectivity

result.label: Primary output · reports
result.value: Timing slack / area / power QoR reports

result.label: Backend handoff
result.value: SDC + SDF for place-and-route and subsequent STA

after: Judge convergence from QoR: Check setup/hold slack at 700 MHz, total area, dynamic power, and leakage against budget. These reports decide whether the synthesis iteration succeeds.
Run logical equivalence checking (LEC): Formally compare RTL with the gate netlist to ensure retiming and optimization preserved function. LEC is a mandatory release gate.
Iterate: Repair violations by refining SDC, changing RTL pipelines, or adjusting library strategy, then resynthesize. QoR often needs many convergence loops.
Hand off to backend: Deliver the LEC-clean netlist with SDC/SDF, libraries, and power intent. After routing, signoff STA with extracted parasitics must reconfirm 700 MHz on real interconnect.
source: In-Datacenter Performance Analysis of a TPU, Jouppi et al., ISCA 2017 (the synthesis flow, constraints, libraries, and QoR methodology are standard EDA practice; tool commands and cycle values are illustrative, not published TPU synthesis data.)
